placements.reasoning: Placed near regresar (0,11), descansar (0,9), and trabajar (0,10) as another common movement/departure action. This preserves the top-row travel/activity cluster and uses an available empty cell.
Ayúdame is most closely anchored to ayudar at (0,4), so it is placed in the same top-row action area. It remains close without disturbing locked items.
Recoger is semantically close to traer (2,11), dar (1,8), and agarrar (1,10)/(2,1) as a take/pick-up action. This nearby open cell keeps handling/transfer verbs clustered.

Placed in a replaceable cell adjacent to ajustar (4,1), romper (4,3), dividir (4,4), and near cambiarse (0,3) as a self-directed fixing/getting-ready action. Using the former arreglar spot matches the repair/fix semantic area well.
Buscar belongs with encontrar (1,1) as the natural search/find pair. This central open cell also sits near esperar (2,7), keeping common process verbs together.
Dejar fits near dar (1,8), guardar (1,9), and traer (2,11) as a transfer/placement action. The position supports a cluster of give/put/leave-related verbs.
Espera is the imperative/interjection counterpart to esperar at (2,7), so adjacent placement best preserves semantic and motor association. This is a strong anchor-based choice.

Estudiar is placed near practicar (6,2) and intentar (6,1) at the broader skill/effort side of the folder, using a far-right open slot that does not disrupt denser action clusters. No closer replaceable cell offered a stronger anchor than preserving other tighter clusters.

Festejar is a near-synonym of celebrar at (7,7), so placing it in the same bottom-row social/affection cluster near disfrutar, abrazar, besar, and pelear is the best semantic fit. This keeps celebratory/social actions grouped.

Manejar is a transport/action verb, placed near moverse (4,0) and regresar (0,11) within the broader movement/action space. The edge slot avoids breaking existing compact clusters while staying in a movement-related row.

Quedarse con is semantically close to guardar (1,9), robar (6,9), and tener (1,11) as a possession/keeping action. A replaceable cell is preferred, and this position preserves more strongly anchored adjacent spaces for other items.
Escapar fits naturally beside atacar (6,11) and near esconderse (2,2) as part of a danger/conflict/avoidance cluster. The replaceable cell is ideal and keeps it near conflict-related actions.
Me prestas is best anchored to dar (1,8), guardar (1,9), and tener (1,11) as a borrowing/lending phrase. This replaceable cell on the same row keeps transfer/possession concepts together.
Recibir is the counterpart to dar (1,8) and relates to traer (2,11) and mandar (3,10). Placing it adjacent to traer creates a clear send/bring/receive grouping.

No is functionally broad rather than a true verb, but in this subfolder it is placed in an open peripheral slot to avoid breaking core semantic verb clusters. This is a deliberate soft-rule compromise due to lack of a strong action anchor in the doing folder.
Rescatar belongs near atacar (6,11), pelear (7,11), and escapar (6,10) in a danger/help/conflict area. The replaceable cell keeps emergency actions grouped and uses a preferred reusable position.

Reparar is strongly anchored to ajustar (4,1) and near construir (3,6), with the replaceable cell formerly holding arruinar creating a useful repair/ruin contrast. This is an excellent semantic reuse of a replaceable slot.
Pagar sits naturally by comprar (0,8), vender (5,9), and rentar (3,9) as part of a commerce cluster. The replaceable cell in column 9 preserves this financial grouping well.
Cargar is close to levantar (2,0), empujar (6,0), empacar (4,10), and agarrar as a carrying/handling action. This replaceable cell keeps it within the physical-manipulation region of the folder.

conflicts.description: Item 72308 "no" is labeled as a verb in the source data, but contextually it functions as an interjection/discourse word rather than a doing-action verb.
conflicts.resolution: Kept the provided POS as already known (no inference override in output), and placed it in a low-interference peripheral empty cell rather than forcing it into a misleading action cluster position among tightly related verbs.
